_comment: Adapter-creek deploy-test exclusions. Merged with Next.js's canonical
Only for tests that fundamentally do not apply to the Cloudflare
- `suites.*.failed` — individual subtests whose assertions check a
specific Node-runtime identity or hit a workerd/miniflare HTTP
transport quirk the adapter can't paper over.
- `rules.exclude` — whole test files whose `next build` fails on
workerd because the fixture requires a native .node addon or a
bundler feature that doesn't work in our pipeline.
Regressions and flakey tests are NOT listed here. If a test starts
failing for a reason other than the ones above, fix the adapter.

failed: undici fetch undici global Headers should return true when undici is used
undici fetch undici global Request should return true when undici is used
undici fetch undici global Response should return true when undici is used
test/e2e/undici-fetch/index.test.ts._note: Runtime-identity probe: expects `globalThis.Headers/Request/Response` to be undici's implementation. workerd ships its own spec-compliant WHATWG classes — behavioral parity is fine, identity isn't. The 4th subtest (`global fetch should return true`) passes.

rules._exclude_note: Turbopack regression at the pinned canary: JSX inside a workspace-pkg `.js` file fails to parse. Webpack builds the same fixture cleanly; vanilla `next build` (default Turbopack) fails identically without our adapter. Nothing actionable at the adapter level — will clear when the upstream canary lands the turbopack-side fix.
